cards.q: Why do minimal tool descriptions cause problems?
cards.a: Tool descriptions are the primary mechanism LLMs use for selection. Descriptions like 'Retrieves customer info' vs 'Retrieves order details' don't differentiate, causing misrouting. Aim for 3-4+ sentences covering what, when, when-NOT, params, edge cases.

cards.q: What should a well-written tool description include?
cards.a: Purpose, expected input formats, example inputs, edge cases, when to use vs similar alternatives, and warnings (rate limits, side effects). Anthropic's guide 'Writing tools for agents' calls description quality the single biggest factor in tool performance.

cards.q: How do you signal a tool error to Claude?
cards.a: Return a tool_result block with is_error: true and a descriptive content string explaining what went wrong (e.g., 'Rate limit exceeded. Retry after 60 seconds'). Claude will retry 2-3 times on parameter errors. Never return generic 'Operation failed.'

cards.q: Why is giving an agent 18 tools worse than giving it 4-5?
cards.a: Too many tools degrades selection reliability by increasing decision complexity. Agents with out-of-scope tools tend to misuse them (a synthesis agent attempting web search). Scope tools to role; provide cross-role tools only for high-frequency needs.

cards.q: What are the four tool_choice values and what do they do?
cards.a: auto (default; Claude may return text), any (must call SOME tool), {type:'tool', name:'...'} (must call this specific tool), none (no tools allowed). Critical: any and named-tool are INCOMPATIBLE with extended/adaptive thinking, only auto/none allowed there.

cards.q: Where do you configure shared team MCP servers vs personal ones?
cards.a: Shared: project-scoped .mcp.json at repo root (committed). Personal/experimental: local scope in ~/.claude.json. The 'local' scope is now the DEFAULT (renamed from 'project' in older docs). 'user' scope (renamed from 'global') applies across all projects but stays personal.

cards.q: What is the env-var substitution syntax in .mcp.json?
cards.a: ${VAR} for required, ${VAR:-default} for default values. Works in command, args, env, url, and headers. Plugin-scoped servers additionally expose ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} and ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}.

cards.q: What MCP transports does Claude Code support, and which is recommended for remote servers?
cards.a: stdio (local processes), http (streamable-http, recommended for remote), sse (deprecated for remote, use http instead). WebSocket is NOT in the 2025-06-18 MCP spec. Configure via 'claude mcp add --transport <type>'.

cards.q: What is the MCP tool naming convention?
cards.a: mcp__<server-name>__<tool-name> with double underscores. Wildcards work in allowedTools (e.g., mcp__github__*). Reserved name: 'workspace' cannot be used as a server name.

cards.q: What are MCP resources, and how do they differ from tools and prompts?
cards.a: Resources are addressable read-mostly data exposed via URIs (issue summaries, schemas, doc hierarchies). Tools are actions/computations (read-write or side-effectful). Prompts are user-triggered templated workflows. Sampling lets a server ask the client to call an LLM (requires user approval).

cards.q: When should you use Grep vs Glob vs Read vs Edit?
cards.a: Grep: search file contents (ripgrep). Glob: find files by name/extension pattern. Read: load full file (PDFs/images/notebooks supported). Edit: targeted modification using unique-text replacement. Use specialized tools over Bash equivalents (find, rg, cat, sed).

cards.q: How do you distinguish access failures from valid empty results?
cards.a: Access failures (timeouts, permission denied) need retry/recovery decisions. Valid empty results mean the query succeeded but matched nothing. Returning the same shape for both hides critical info from the coordinator. Use is_error: true for failures; empty array with success metadata for valid empties.

cards.q: What does strict: true do on a tool definition?
cards.a: Guarantees the model's tool input is schema-valid (eliminates structural JSON errors). Combine with tool_choice: 'any' to guarantee both a tool call AND schema validity. Limits: max 20 strict tools per request, 24 optional params total, 16 union-typed params.

cards.q: What's wrong with replacing fetch_url with a generic tool?
cards.a: Generic tools lack constraints. Better to use constrained alternatives like load_document that validate document URLs, preventing misuse on arbitrary URLs. Constraints in the tool surface > constraints in the prompt.

cards.q: Anthropic's tool granularity guidance for related operations?
cards.a: Consolidate related ops (create_pr / review_pr / merge_pr) into ONE tool with an 'action' parameter. Fewer, more capable tools reduce selection ambiguity. But avoid kitchen-sink tools, granularity is favored over both micro-fragmentation AND giant catch-alls.

cards.q: Community vs custom MCP server, when to choose which?
cards.a: Use community/registered MCP servers for standard integrations (Jira, GitHub, Slack); reserve custom servers for team-specific workflows community solutions don't cover. Vet third-party servers, they're a prompt-injection vector via untrusted content.

cards.q: What is the latest text editor tool ID and what changed?
cards.a: text_editor_20250728. Commands: view, str_replace, create, insert. The undo_edit command was REMOVED in text_editor_20250429. New max_characters parameter added. Schema is built into the model, you don't define one.

crash.val: #1 selection factor. 3-4+ sentences: what, when, when-NOT, params, edge cases. Differentiate similar tools clearly.
Required: name (regex ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{1,64}$), description, input_schema. Optional: input_examples, strict, cache_control.
auto (may return text), any (must call SOME tool), {type:'tool',name:...} (force specific), none (no tools).
any and named-tool are INCOMPATIBLE with extended/adaptive thinking. Only auto/none allowed when thinking on.
Return tool_result with is_error: true + descriptive content. Include errorCategory (transient/validation/business/permission) and isRetryable. Claude retries 2-3x on param errors.
4-5 per agent target. >18 degrades selection. Scope tools to role; provide cross-role only for high-frequency needs.
Consolidate related ops with an action param (avoid kitchen-sink AND micro-fragmentation). Split monoliths when truly distinct.
http (recommended for remote), sse (DEPRECATED for remote), stdio (local). WebSocket NOT in 2025-06-18 spec.
local (default, was 'project') in ~/.claude.json. project (shared via .mcp.json). user (was 'global').
${VAR} or ${VAR:-default} in command, args, env, url, headers. Plugin scope: ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}, ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}.
mcp__<server>__<tool> (double underscores). Wildcards work in allowedTools (e.g. mcp__github__*). workspace reserved.
Resources = addressable read-mostly data (URIs, schemas, catalogs). Tools = actions/computations. Prompts = user-triggered templated workflows. Sampling = server asks client to call LLM.
Grep (content), Glob (filenames), Read (full file incl. PDF/img), Edit (unique-text), Write, Bash, Agent, WebFetch, WebSearch, ToolSearch, AskUserQuestion.
Built-in tool letting Claude pause in-loop to ask the user a multiple-choice question. Use for clarifying ambiguous intent or confirming destructive actions before proceeding.
strict: true guarantees schema-valid input. Combine with tool_choice: 'any' for forced + valid. Limits: 20 strict tools, 24 optional params, 16 unions.
Prefer registered/community MCP for standard integrations (Jira, GitHub). Custom only for team-specific workflows. Vet third-party (prompt-injection vector).
Subagents try transient retries locally. Only propagate errors they can't resolve (with what was attempted + partial results).
"Retrieves customer info" vs "Retrieves order details" causes misrouting. Expand to 3-4+ sentences.
"Operation failed" hides type, retryability. Always return structured error metadata.

crash.key: Same shape access fail vs empty
crash.val: Distinguish access failures (timeouts, perm denied) from valid empty results. Same shape hides info from coordinator.

examQs.q: Your agent has two similar tools: get_customer ('Retrieves customer information') and lookup_order ('Retrieves order details'). The agent frequently picks the wrong one. What's the best first step?
opts: Add 5-8 few-shot examples demonstrating correct tool selection patterns
Expand each tool's description to 3-4+ sentences covering input formats, example queries, edge cases, and clear boundaries for when to use each vs the other
Build a routing layer that parses user input and pre-selects tools based on detected keywords
Consolidate both into a single lookup_entity tool that internally routes queries
examQs.explain: Tool descriptions are the primary mechanism LLMs use for selection. Anthropic's tool-writing guide calls description quality the single biggest factor in tool performance. Expanding descriptions is the highest-leverage, lowest-effort fix. Few-shot adds token overhead. A routing layer is over-engineered. Consolidation is a valid pattern for related ops but more effort than needed when descriptions are the issue.

examQs.q: The web search subagent times out. Which error propagation approach best enables the coordinator to recover intelligently?
opts: Return structured error context including failure type, the attempted query, any partial results, and potential alternative approaches
Implement automatic retry with exponential backoff, returning a generic 'search unavailable' status after all retries are exhausted
Catch the timeout and return an empty result set marked as successful
Propagate the timeout exception directly to a top-level handler that terminates the research workflow
examQs.explain: Structured error context gives the coordinator the information it needs: whether to retry with a modified query, try alternative approaches, or proceed with partial results. Generic statuses hide context, marking failure as success suppresses the error, and terminating the whole workflow wastes completed work.

examQs.q: You need to add a Jira integration for your team's agent. There's an existing community MCP server for Jira and you could also build a custom one. What's the right choice?
opts: Use the community MCP server for standard Jira integration, reserving custom servers for team-specific workflows
Build a custom server to ensure full control over the integration and future extensibility
Use the community server but wrap it in a proxy layer for additional logging and monitoring
Build a custom server that exactly mirrors the community server's API for consistency with other custom integrations
examQs.explain: For standard integrations, registered/community MCP servers are preferred over custom implementations. Custom servers should be reserved for team-specific workflows community solutions don't cover. Building custom when community works wastes development effort. (Do still vet third-party servers for prompt-injection risk.)

examQs.q: Your agent uses a broad analyze_dependencies tool alongside built-in Grep. Despite the custom tool being more capable, the agent consistently defaults to Grep. How do you fix this?
opts: Split analyze_dependencies into granular tools (list_imports, resolve_transitive_deps, detect_circular_deps) with detailed descriptions of when to prefer each over Grep
Remove Grep from the agent's available tools to force it to use the custom tool
Add a system prompt instruction telling the agent to prefer custom tools over built-in ones
Rename analyze_dependencies to something more descriptive like smart_dependency_analyzer
examQs.explain: Granular, single-purpose tools with explicit descriptions are easier for the model to select correctly. Removing Grep sacrifices legitimate use cases. Prompt instructions are unreliable. Renaming alone doesn't solve the description quality problem. (Anthropic's broader guidance: consolidate related ops with an 'action' param, but in this case granularity is the right call because the operations are conceptually distinct.)

examQs.q: You want Opus 4.7 to (a) force-call extract_metadata first AND (b) use adaptive thinking to plan the rest of the pipeline. Your API call returns an error. Why?
opts: tool_choice: {type:'tool', name:...} or 'any' is incompatible with extended/adaptive thinking, only 'auto' or 'none' allowed when thinking is on
Adaptive thinking requires the 1-hour cache TTL but your tools block uses 5-minute caching
The strict: true flag is required on extract_metadata when thinking is enabled
extract_metadata must be the only tool in the request when forcing tool_choice
examQs.explain: tool_choice 'any' or named-tool is incompatible with extended/adaptive thinking, the API only allows 'auto' or 'none' when thinking is enabled. To get both behaviors, split into two API calls: first call with thinking off and tool_choice forcing extract_metadata; second call with thinking on for planning. Or use a hook to enforce ordering. The other options describe non-existent constraints.
